name: Manama
summary: Manama is normally considered very safe for tourists with low crime rates and friendly locals, but the current regional conflict involving Iran and geopolitical tensions have significantly elevated security risks. The US State Department has issued a Level 3 travel advisory due to ongoing threats of missile and drone attacks.
quickVerdict: Due to the current armed conflict between the United States and Iran and associated regional tensions, Manama faces elevated security risks including potential drone and missile attacks. While the city traditionally enjoys low crime rates and is normally safe for tourists, the exceptional circumstances require extreme caution. US and other Western citizens are advised to shelter in place or consider departure via land routes to Saudi Arabia, as the airport remains closed to commercial flights. Travel should be postponed unless absolutely essential.

neighborhoods.name: Juffair
neighborhoods.description: Popular expatriate area with numerous bars, restaurants, and international businesses. Generally considered very safe with high security presence and well-lit streets.

neighborhoods.name: Seef District
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial and residential area with luxury hotels, malls, and upscale amenities. Well-patrolled and safe for tourists day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Diplomatic Area
neighborhoods.description: Financial district housing embassies, banks, and corporate headquarters. Heavily secured with excellent infrastructure and minimal crime risk.

neighborhoods.name: Manama Souq Area
neighborhoods.description: Historic market district where most petty crime occurs. Pickpocketing and smartphone theft are more common here, especially during busy periods.

neighborhoods.name: Bab Al Bahrain
neighborhoods.description: Historic gateway and popular tourist area with shops and restaurants. Generally safe but can be crowded with increased risk of minor scams and pickpocketing.

neighborhoods.name: Old Manama Districts
neighborhoods.description: Older residential areas that can be less safe at night with poor lighting and fewer security patrols. Best avoided after dark, especially for solo travelers.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Scam
scams.description: Some taxi drivers don't use meters or take longer routes to inflate fares, particularly targeting tourists unfamiliar with the city.
scams.howToAvoid: Always insist on using the meter or agree on a fixed fare beforehand. Use ride-hailing apps like Uber or Careem for transparent pricing.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guide
scams.description: Individuals pose as official guides offering tours at inflated prices or leading tourists to overpriced shops where they receive commissions.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use guides from reputable tour companies or hotels. Verify credentials and ask for identification before agreeing to any tours.

scams.name: Fake Jewelry in Souqs
scams.description: Vendors in markets may sell counterfeit gemstones or jewelry claiming they are genuine precious stones at seemingly bargain prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy only from established, reputable dealers. Be very wary of deals that seem too good to be true and consider having items authenticated.

scams.name: Overpriced Restaurant Menu
scams.description: Some tourist-area restaurants present menus with inflated prices compared to what locals pay, particularly in areas near major attractions.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices clearly before ordering and choose restaurants that display their prices prominently. Ask locals for recommendations.

soloFemale.overview: Under normal circumstances, Manama is relatively safe for solo female travelers due to its progressive nature compared to other Gulf countries. However, current regional tensions and security alerts require extreme caution. Women should dress modestly, be mindful of local customs, and currently consider postponing travel due to ongoing security risks.
tips: Dress conservatively with shoulders and knees covered, especially in traditional areas
Stay in well-lit, populated areas and avoid isolated districts at night
Use reputable taxi services or ride-hailing apps rather than walking alone after dark

nightSafety.overview: Manama traditionally has good nighttime safety with low violent crime rates and active nightlife areas that are well-patrolled. However, current security alerts recommend staying indoors and avoiding unnecessary movement due to regional conflict risks including potential missile and drone attacks.
tips: Currently follow shelter-in-place guidelines issued by local authorities and embassies
Stay in secure accommodation and avoid unnecessary nighttime travel during current tensions
If you must travel, use licensed taxis or ride-hailing apps and stay in well-lit areas

transport.metro: Bahrain does not have a metro system. Public transport consists of a bus network operated by Bahrain Public Transport Company serving major areas including the airport and tourist attractions.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Careem operate throughout Manama and are considered safe and reliable options. Apps provide transparent pricing and tracking features for added security.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are identifiable by orange fenders and yellow license plates. Generally safe but always use meter or agree on fare beforehand. Some unlicensed taxis may overcharge.
transport.tips: Currently, commercial flights are suspended from Bahrain International Airport due to regional conflict. Land routes to Saudi Arabia remain open for those needing to depart. Use established ride-hailing services for local transport.

customs: Use your right hand for greetings, eating, and passing objects as the left hand is considered unclean
Dress modestly in public with shoulders and knees covered, especially in religious and traditional areas
Respect Islamic customs during prayer times and avoid eating or drinking in public during Ramadan daylight hours

health.overview: Bahrain has excellent healthcare infrastructure with modern hospitals and medical facilities. The country spends 4.5% of GDP on healthcare and provides high-quality services comparable to international standards.
health.water: Tap water is generally safe to drink as it meets quality standards, though many visitors prefer bottled water due to taste differences from mineral content.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for most travelers. Routine vaccinations should be up to date. Check CDC recommendations for any recent health alerts.
health.altitude: Sea-level location with desert climate. Summer temperatures can be extreme (over 40°C/104°F) with high humidity. Stay hydrated and limit outdoor exposure during peak heat.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Manama: +973-1724-2700 (currently operating with limited services due to security situation). Emergency: ManamaConsular@state.gov or https://mytravel.state.gov/s/crisis-intake

faq.a: Under normal circumstances, Manama is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and friendly locals. However, current regional tensions and armed conflict involving Iran have created elevated security risks. The US State Department advises reconsidering travel due to threats of drone and missile attacks.

Traditionally, Manama is quite safe at night with active nightlife and good security. However, current security advisories recommend staying indoors and following shelter-in-place guidance due to ongoing regional conflict and potential attacks.

faq.q: Is Manama safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Manama is normally relatively safe for solo female travelers and is considered one of the more progressive Gulf cities. However, current security situation requires extreme caution. Women should dress modestly, stay aware of surroundings, and consider postponing travel until tensions subside.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Manama?
faq.a: Avoid older districts of Manama at night due to poor lighting. The souq area has higher petty crime rates. Currently, all non-essential movement should be avoided due to security alerts about potential missile and drone attacks.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Manama?
faq.a: Public buses and licensed taxis are generally safe and reliable. Ride-hailing apps like Uber and Careem are recommended for transparency and tracking. However, commercial flights are currently suspended, and overland routes to Saudi Arabia are the only departure option during current crisis.
